reviewers.question: Does the privacy model hold — can any member read another member's private handoff through any path (MCP tool, raw PostgREST, markdown files)?
reviewers.evidence: MCP path: OwnerScopeProxy scopes all handoffs reads (DEFAULT_SCOPED_COLLECTIONS); team-e2e.mjs 'bob cannot see alice private self-handoff' green. DB path: 012 RLS policy identical contract to 011 (owner_id/shared_owner_id JWT claims, fails closed on NULL); hardened e2e 'bob direct REST sees ZERO alice private handoffs' green live. Create-time guard: private + to_member≠self rejected with explanatory error so no silently-invisible handoffs exist.

reviewers.question: Can the evidence payload (tried objects) be corrupted by any backend's serialization?
reviewers.evidence: Markdown: was corrupted ([object Object]) — fixed in stringifyScalar/parseScalar with JSON roundtrip; tests/markdown-roundtrip.test.ts covers objects, mixed lists, nested arrays, invalid-JSON fallback, plain-scalar regression. Supabase: jsonb native — live probe 'evidence fields intact over supabase' green. PocketBase: json field type in 009_handoffs.js mirror.

reviewers.question: Do lifecycle timestamps behave correctly under re-transition and cross-member updates?
reviewers.evidence: handoff-update.ts:47-53 reads existing record first (getOne through OwnerScopeProxy — cross-owner returns RECORD_NOT_FOUND before any write) and only stamps accepted_at/completed_at when not already set. Unit tests cover stamp-once; e2e covers bob (recipient, shared scope) performing accept+complete.

reviewers.question: Is any frozen contract broken — published-version test counts, dist canon, setup-status/setup-migrate sync?
reviewers.evidence: mvp-test-plan.mjs untouched (still 41, pins npm 0.8.0). verify-dist PASS fatal=0. handoffs added to BOTH setup-status EXPECTED_COLLECTIONS and setup-migrate COLLECTION_SCHEMAS in the same diff (2026-05-11 incident regression guard). smoke-test.mjs (local dist, CI) correctly bumped to 44.

notes: handoff_update does not allow changing to_member or owner_scope, so the create-time private/recipient invariant cannot be violated post-create. supersedes/task_id/session_ids are freeform links by design (matches sessions.task_id precedent).
